mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate raises the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in tightening periods are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp monthly increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings revisions, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5d returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in real GDP and housing starts amplifies consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days through reinforced demand-side momentum.
failure_condition: Joint signal produces negative Rank IC over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not exceed baseline by >1.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production indicates robust manufacturing output and supply chain normalization, supporting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon during post-shock recovery periods by easing input cost pressures.
failure_condition: Forward returns in recovery periods do not exceed baseline by >1.5%, or Rank IC falls below 0.02 over a 2-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation readings force market pricing of prolonged monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as real yields rise.
failure_condition: 60d forward returns remain positive when CPIAUCSL > 230, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Expanding real consumer credit balances reflect stronger household spending capacity, directly supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days through immediate earnings visibility.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains negative across all rolling windows, or 5d forward returns show no statistical difference from baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in unemployment and increases in nonfarm payrolls confirm labor market tightening, reducing wage-price spiral fears and boosting equity valuations over a 20-day horizon through improved macro stability.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict positive returns in > 40% of observations, or interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during a monetary tightening cycle signal demand destruction and higher borrowing costs, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as construction and related sectors contract.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are not significantly lower when HOUST falls during rate hikes, or forward returns remain positive in >50% of triggered cases.
